title: Day 4: Pre-commit Triple (ledger-append + human-lint + sync-meta) via Husky Template
description: Complete the pre-commit enforcement pipeline: fully implement fab ledger-append (derives one intent entry from git diff --cached, appends to .intent-ledger.jsonl) and fab human-lint (compares @HUMAN section hashes in .fabric/human-lock.json against current file content; mismatched sections cause exit 1). Finalize the husky pre-commit template with the 3-step pipeline (sync-meta --check-only → human-lint → ledger-append) with total runtime < 300ms. Add the agents.meta.json edit guard that blocks direct modification unless FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT=1. Total runtime constraint < 300ms is critical for developer experience.

files.change: Run git diff --cached; parse diff for changed file paths + insertion/deletion summary; derive intent string; create LedgerEntry { commit_sha: 'staged', intent, affected_paths, timestamp }; append JSON line to .intent-ledger.jsonl; create file if not exists

Read .fabric/human-lock.json; for each entry extract file content between start_line and end_line; compute sha256; compare against stored hash; on any mismatch exit 1 with message showing file + line range + expected vs actual hash

3-step pipeline: 1) npx fab sync-meta --check-only; 2) npx fab human-lint || exit 1; 3) npx fab ledger-append --staged; 4) agents.meta.json edit guard (grep staged files, block without FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT=1)
Initial schema/example for human-lock.json: array of { file, start_line, end_line, hash } entries; created by fab sync-meta when @HUMAN sections detected
Add @HUMAN section detection: scan AGENTS.md files for @HUMAN sections; extract line ranges; compute sha256; write to .fabric/human-lock.json alongside agents.meta.json update
spawnGit helper; getStagedFiles(); getStagedDiffSummary(); used by ledger-append
computeFileHash(path, startLine?, endLine?): string using Node crypto sha256; used by human-lint and sync-meta

implementation: Implement git utilities in utils/git.ts: spawnGit(args) using child_process.spawnSync; getStagedFiles() returns string[]; getStagedDiffSummary() returns { added, deleted, files } summary object
Implement hash utilities in utils/hash.ts: computeFileHash(filePath) for whole-file sha256; computeSectionHash(filePath, startLine, endLine) for line-range sha256 using Node fs + crypto

Complete ledger-append.ts: use getStagedDiffSummary() to build LedgerEntry; append as single JSON line to .intent-ledger.jsonl using fs.appendFileSync; create file if not exists; handle empty staged diff gracefully (no-op, no error)

Complete human-lint.ts: read .fabric/human-lock.json (skip if not exists — means no @HUMAN sections declared); for each entry call computeSectionHash; compare to stored hash; collect all mismatches; if any mismatches found, print each with file:startLine-endLine format and exit 1

Update sync-meta.ts: after updating node hashes, scan each AGENTS.md for @HUMAN section markers (## @HUMAN or @HUMAN comment blocks); extract line numbers; update .fabric/human-lock.json entries; compute sha256 of each section
Write final templates/husky/pre-commit script with 4 guards in order; benchmark on realistic commit (target < 300ms total); document timing in pre-commit comments
Create .fabric/human-lock.json with documented schema (empty initial state with schema comment)
Write integration test: create test AGENTS.md with @HUMAN section; run fab sync-meta; manually edit @HUMAN section; run fab human-lint — must exit 1

reference.pattern: Append-only JSONL ledger; line-range sha256 for section integrity; git pre-commit hook pattern
reference.examples: mcp-first-fabric.md §4.4 for complete pre-commit pipeline; §3 for human-lock.json schema; §4.1 for @HUMAN section format; Node crypto createHash('sha256') pattern

criteria: fab ledger-append --staged derives exactly one LedgerEntry per git commit invocation; .intent-ledger.jsonl grows by exactly 1 line per pre-commit run; file created if not exists

fab human-lint compares sha256 hashes in .fabric/human-lock.json against current @HUMAN section content; any single-character change in @HUMAN section triggers exit 1 with file:line range error message
husky pre-commit hook runs all 3 commands (sync-meta --check-only, human-lint, ledger-append) in sequence; total measured runtime < 300ms on a 10-file commit in a realistic project
Direct edit to .fabric/agents.meta.json without FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT=1 environment variable set is blocked by pre-commit hook with exit 1 and descriptive error message
Intentional edit to an @HUMAN section in AGENTS.md followed by git commit attempt is blocked with human-lint error identifying the exact file and line range

rationale.chosen_approach: git diff --cached for staged-only ledger entries (not working tree); line-range sha256 for @HUMAN protection (exact string match, not AST); spawnSync for git calls (synchronous, simpler in pre-commit context)
alternatives_considered: AST-based @HUMAN section detection (more robust but adds parser dependency and latency)
Full working-tree diff for ledger (would include unstaged changes, confusing semantics)
Process.env check in CLI command for FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT (chosen for simplicity over file-lock approach)
decision_factors: < 300ms budget requires synchronous git calls and minimal I/O
Line-range sha256 is deterministic and fast; AST would add > 100ms per file
Append-only JSONL is immutable audit log; any other format risks corruption
rationale.tradeoffs: Line-range sha256 is brittle if file encoding or line endings change (CRLF vs LF); document that .gitattributes should normalize line endings

manual_checks: Stage a file change; run fab ledger-append --staged; cat .intent-ledger.jsonl — must have exactly 1 new line
Create AGENTS.md with @HUMAN section; run fab sync-meta; inspect .fabric/human-lock.json — must contain entry with file + line range + sha256 hash
Edit 1 character in @HUMAN section; run fab human-lint — must exit 1 with file:line range error
Restore @HUMAN section; run fab human-lint — must exit 0
Stage a change to .fabric/agents.meta.json; run pre-commit hook without FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT — must exit 1
Set FAB_ALLOW_META_EDIT=1; stage same change; run pre-commit hook — must pass
success_metrics: pre-commit runtime < 300ms measured on 10-file staged commit
100% of @HUMAN section mutations detected by human-lint

risks.description: 300ms budget may be tight on slow machines with many AGENTS.md files
risks.mitigation: Use spawnSync for git calls; read only staged files in ledger-append (not full tree); benchmark early and optimize if needed
risks.fallback: Allow configurable timeout in .fabric/config.json; document performance expectations

risks.description: CRLF vs LF line ending differences will cause sha256 mismatches for @HUMAN sections across OS
risks.mitigation: Normalize to LF before computing sha256; document .gitattributes requirement; add line in README

interfaces.name: LedgerEntry
interfaces.definition: { commit_sha: string; intent: string; affected_paths: string[]; timestamp: string; author?: string }

interfaces.name: HumanLockEntry
interfaces.definition: { file: string; start_line: number; end_line: number; hash: string }

key_functions.purpose: Append JSON line to .intent-ledger.jsonl using fs.appendFileSync

key_functions.signature: validateHumanLock(projectRoot: string): { valid: boolean; violations: HumanLockViolation[] }
key_functions.purpose: Check all human-lock entries against current file content
